objective: Implement PU-014 by hardening PR, CI, review, Linear, and stale-state closeout refresh so external-state and review-state packets carry verifier-owned fetch proof and pr-closeout blocks unsupported remote/review/tracker delivery claims separately.
deepModuleBoundary: src/lib/external-state, src/lib/review-state, src/lib/delivery-truth, and src/lib/pr-closeout closeout-claim evaluation only
implementationThesis: Use additive packet proof fields and closeout blocker expansion: external-state-snapshot/v1 and review-state/v1 must prove who fetched the live state, where the fetched artifact is referenced, and the artifact hash. pr-closeout must treat remote checks, review threads, Linear alignment, and merge readiness as separate delivery-truth verdicts so stale or missing external truth cannot be hidden behind local validation or a single blended readiness claim.

currentEvidence.observed: external-state-snapshot/v1 already separates GitHub PR, checks, reviews, CodeRabbit, Linear, and CircleCI sources with TTL, fetchedAt, headSha, evidenceUse, freshness, resultStatus, and staleReasons.
currentEvidence.gap: the packet does not yet require verifier-owned fetch proof such as fetchReceiptRef, fetchedArtifactHash, and verifierIdentity.

currentEvidence.observed: claim support already rejects stale snapshots, non-claim-support evidence, source staleness, non-passing source status, missing head SHA, and head SHA mismatch.
currentEvidence.gap: fresh-looking metadata without verifier-owned fetch proof is not currently a blocker.

currentEvidence.observed: review-state/v1 already separates GitHub reviews, CodeRabbit status, unresolved review threads, and reviewer artifact receipts from CI and tracker state.
currentEvidence.gap: the packet itself does not yet carry fetchReceiptRef, fetchedArtifactHash, or verifierIdentity for the review-state fetch.

currentEvidence.observed: reviewer artifact receipts are validated for kind, ref, headSha, expected producer, and non-zero size.
currentEvidence.gap: review-state packets can validate without proving the live review-state snapshot was fetched by a verifier-owned path.

currentEvidence.observed: PR closeout currently blocks delivery-truth verdicts for merge_ready and root_surface_tidy.
currentEvidence.gap: remote_checks_current, review_threads_resolved, and linear_state_aligned delivery verdicts are not separately closeout-blocking.

currentEvidence.observed: delivery-truth currently models root_surface_tidy, goal_ready_for_judge_pm, and merge_ready, with merge_ready requiring separate evidence families in composition tests.
currentEvidence.gap: PU-014 closeout claims for current remote checks, review resolution, and Linear alignment need a typed delivery-truth claim surface before closeout can block them independently.

outOfScope: creating a new public GitHub, Linear, or CodeRabbit connector command unless existing code proves no narrower module path can express PU-014
performing external writes to GitHub, Linear, or CodeRabbit
treating gh CLI output as claim-supporting evidence unless it is converted into a verifier-owned receipt-backed packet
changing harness next from advisory cockpit into executable authority
embedding full PR comments, raw review bodies, transcripts, prompts, secrets, credentials, or bulky telemetry in closeout packets
claiming merge readiness, goal completion, or cleanup readiness from local validation alone
acceptanceCriteria: external-state-snapshot/v1 validation rejects packets without fetchReceiptRef, fetchedArtifactHash, or verifierIdentity.
review-state/v1 validation rejects packets without fetchReceiptRef, fetchedArtifactHash, or verifierIdentity.

external-state claim support returns a distinct blocker when otherwise-current metadata lacks verifier-owned fetch proof or when fetchReceiptRef, fetchedArtifactHash, and verifierIdentity are not cross-bound to the same verifier-owned fetched artifact.

delivery-truth types in src/lib/delivery-truth/types.ts define canonical PU-014 claim keys remote_checks_current, review_threads_resolved, and linear_state_aligned; src/lib/pr-closeout/delivery-truth.ts treats those exact keys as closeout-blocking alongside merge_ready and root_surface_tidy.

pr-closeout blocks remote_checks_current, review_threads_resolved, linear_state_aligned, merge_ready, and root_surface_tidy independently when any supplied verdict is failed, blocked, unknown, missing, stale, or not claim-supporting.

tests cover stale head SHA, failing CI, stale Linear state, unresolved review threads, missing CodeRabbit/reviewer proof where applicable, missing fetchReceiptRef, forged or mismatched fetch proof, and fresh-looking metadata without verifier-owned fetch proof.

external-state and review-state packets carry PR identity binding for repo, prNumber, and headSha where PR context applies; mismatches against the active closeout target classify as blocked_stale_external_context or the closest existing explicit stale-context blocker.

before any closeout verdict generation, external-state and review-state fetch proof must be refreshed or revalidated inside the accepted TTL window; stale triage observations cannot support claim verdicts.
no closeout path can turn local validation, remote checks, review threads, tracker state, and merge readiness into one blended success state.

live PR orientation resolves the target PR from the current branch/head when possible; fixed PR #305 commands are allowed only as this thread's orientation evidence and cannot become claim support unless transformed into receipt-backed external-state/review-state packets with matching repo, PR number, and headSha.

automationPlan.name: intent artifact JSON validation
proves: PU-014 intent artifact is syntactically valid JSON before review

automationPlan.name: focused PU-014 verifier tests
automationPlan.command: pnpm vitest run src/lib/pr-closeout.test.ts src/lib/pr-closeout/*.test.ts src/lib/external-state/*.test.ts src/lib/review-state/*.test.ts src/lib/delivery-truth/*.test.ts
proves: PR closeout, external-state, review-state, and delivery-truth verifier behavior for PU-014

automationPlan.name: live PR metadata orientation
proves: current PR #305 orientation is known before making any closeout claim

automationPlan.name: live PR checks orientation
proves: current visible PR #305 check state is known before making any closeout claim

automationPlan.name: goal board validation
proves: goal state and receipts remain valid after PU-014 updates

reviewPlan.artifactNamingNote: PU-specific review artifact names intentionally override default reviewer filenames so the goal board can tie findings to this lifecycle unit.

postReviewMutationPolicy: any material mutation to objective, implementationThesis, currentEvidence, inScope, outOfScope, guardedPathGlobs, acceptanceCriteria, automationPlan, reviewPlan, stopConditions, or rollback resets reviewStatus to pending

parallelPrTriageStrategy.default: allow a read-only PR triage subagent to monitor PR #305 while the coordinator performs PU-014 intent review and implementation on the active branch
parallelPrTriageStrategy.parallelCodeWorkAllowed: allowed only when PR #305 blockers are external, draft/review-process only, or unrelated to files touched by PU-014
blockedWhen: PR #305 reports a current-head failure in external-state, review-state, delivery-truth, pr-closeout, or goal-board surfaces touched by PU-014
intent reviewers identify a material closeout authority, stale-state, or blended-truth risk not patched in intent
implementation would require external writes, self-approval, or public command exposure beyond the plan
parallelPrTriageStrategy.safeFallback: continue read-only PR triage, fixture design, and intent refinement without touching production closeout code

stopConditions: Stop if adversarial or agent-native intent review reports a material blocker and the intent has not been patched and re-reviewed.
Stop if a fresh-looking external-state or review-state packet can pass validation without verifier-owned fetch proof.
Stop if pr-closeout can report readiness from one blended local/remote/review/tracker verdict.
Stop if local validation is treated as evidence for remote checks, review threads, Linear state, or merge readiness.
Stop if gh CLI orientation output is treated as claim support without a receipt-backed packet.
Stop if a verifier-owned fetch proof field is accepted by presence alone without cross-binding fetchReceiptRef, fetchedArtifactHash, verifierIdentity, and the fetched artifact/receipt relationship.
Stop if the external-state or review-state PR identity does not match the active closeout target repository, PR number, and headSha.
Stop if implementation uses stale parallel triage observations without a final TTL/headSha refresh before closeout verdict generation.
Stop if a stale head SHA, stale TTL, failed CI/check source, unresolved thread, or missing reviewer ownership can still produce a passing closeout verdict.
Stop if implementation needs to mutate Codex source or external services outside the PU-014 Harness-only contract.
rollback: Remove added verifier-owned fetch proof fields only if the packet contract is replaced by an equivalent stricter receipt-backed fetch proof.
Revert PR closeout delivery-truth blocker expansion if tests prove the expanded claims create duplicate blockers without adding independent closeout protection.
Preserve existing merge_ready separate-evidence composition tests unless replaced by stronger PU-014 tests.
Do not modify or roll back /Users/jamiecraik/dev/codex because PU-014 is Harness-only.
assumptions: PR #305 can remain draft while PU-014 proceeds because draft status blocks merge readiness but does not block local contract hardening.
Existing external-state, review-state, delivery-truth, and pr-closeout modules are the correct deep-module placement for PU-014.
gh pr view and gh pr checks are orientation tools in this slice, not sufficient claim-support evidence by themselves.
The first PU-014 production patch should prefer contract and fixture hardening over introducing a new connector surface unless code evidence proves one is already available and narrow.
